summary: 74 active beads carry no epic parent; most map cleanly to an existing epic or to one of four strongly-implied missing epics (config/runtime-prefs, ops-resilience, ecosystem-interop/origin-breadth, substrate-consolidation). The dependency graph is acyclic under blocks/directed edges (the only 'cycles' are benign symmetric relates-to registrations), but has three genuinely dangling deps (mhx.4/.5/.6 point at nonexistent slug ids emb-targets/emb-eval) and a large tail of stale blocker edges to already-closed beads. Epic-health red flag: 1xc (Scale-hardening, P1) is an OPEN epic with ZERO children while its exact scope (xy95, 6wnh, ma2, 0ns, xgw, fie) sits unparented.

inconsistencies.evidence: dependency depends_on_id='emb-targets' is not a bead id in the dataset (no such id). mhx.5 also blocks 'emb-targets'; mhx.6 blocks 'emb-eval' — both nonexistent.
inconsistencies.fix: Repoint to the real sibling beads: 'emb-targets' == mhx.2 ('Embedding target policy: what gets a vector...'); 'emb-eval' == mhx.3 ('Retrieval quality eval lane...'). These are dangling slug placeholders that never got rewritten to canonical ids, so the mhx internal ordering is unenforced.

inconsistencies.evidence: blocks 'emb-eval' — no bead with that id exists.
inconsistencies.fix: Retarget to polylogue-mhx.3 (retrieval quality eval lane), the intended prerequisite.

inconsistencies.evidence: Epic 'Scale-hardening: bugs that only bite on real-scale archives' is open, P1, has_design=true, has_acc=true, but has 0 children. Meanwhile xy95 (usage diagnostic D-stated on the live 38GB archive), 6wnh (thread refresh on large Codex appends), ma2 (FK index for large web_content_constructs cleanup), 0ns (bound embedding work in large sessions), xgw (schema hygiene) and fie (scaling doctrine) are all unparented and match this epic's AC verbatim.
inconsistencies.fix: Reparent the scale-bite orphans under 1xc so the epic stops reading as an empty promise; its acceptance can only be evaluated once its implied children are attached.

inconsistencies.evidence: 27m blocks polylogue-doh which is CLOSED ('Schema evolution v2: additive migrations'). ze5, 703, b5l, 6wnh, ma2, dab, 90y, ahqd, caq, c04 similarly still carry blocks-edges onto closed beads (doh/4bu/3wb/6h7/27p/x5l).
inconsistencies.fix: Sweep satisfied blocker edges whose target is closed. Harmless if the tracker treats closed deps as satisfied, but they pollute `bd ready`/graph traversal and hide which prerequisites are genuinely open.

inconsistencies.evidence: BLOCKS/directed cycle check is clean, but relates-to is registered bidirectionally, producing apparent cycles s7ae<->37t.11 and 38x<->{da1,83u,5hf,fnm,5wp,jnj,4ts}. These are not real dependency cycles.
inconsistencies.fix: No action needed for scheduling; note for any tooling that walks the dep graph — filter type=='relates-to' before cycle/topo analysis or it will report false cycles.

dep_priority.detail: 37t.11 is 'Context scheduler: one arbiter for everything that enters an agent context', yet it is referenced only by s7ae/s7ae.3 (relates-to). Every bead that injects into agent context bypasses the arbiter in the graph: 3gd (activation chapter+injected state), bfv (advisory PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit injection), 1hj (blackboard delivery via injection), rvh (SRS lesson re-injection), mhx.4 (semantic recall leg in context compilation), gjg (post-compaction re-grounding), 1jc (learned-defaults candidates).
dep_priority.proposed: Add relates-to (or blocks, where 37t.11 must define the budget/arbitration first) from 3gd, bfv, 1hj, rvh, mhx.4, gjg, 1jc to 37t.11 so the scheduler is the declared choke point instead of an implicit one.

dep_priority.detail: bby.11 ('Webui architecture v2: the stack that can carry the ambition') is referenced only by d4zk. Web-surface beads that must build ON the v2 stack do not declare it: bby.8 (web reader perceived perf), 20d.13 (SSE push for live UIs), 90y (in-page overlay), b1n (webui posting), yrx (web Changes tab), scd (web deep links/copy-as-command).
dep_priority.proposed: Add relates-to from bby.8, 20d.13, 90y, b1n, yrx, scd to bby.11 so web work sequences against the architecture decision rather than racing it.

dep_priority.detail: P1 epic with zero children; its AC (scale-only bugs) is covered by unparented orphans. An epic whose children don't exist cannot have its acceptance evaluated.
dep_priority.proposed: Attach xy95, xgw, fie (+ non-slice 6wnh, ma2, 0ns) as children; keep 1xc P1.

dep_priority.detail: t46 ('Contracts own surfaces: delete parallel dispatch and the QA middle layer') is open P2 with a single child, while parallel-dispatch/God-module refactors sit orphaned: 7le (three session->HTML paths), 1fp (5.2k-line facade God-object), 703 (three status assemblies disagree in prod).
dep_priority.proposed: Reparent 7le, 1fp, 703 under t46; they are the concrete 'delete parallel dispatch' work the epic names.

dep_priority.detail: h6r ('Agent identity: stable who-did-this tuple', P2) is a declared prerequisite of h10 (prediction/calibration) and underlies per-model/per-config partitioning used by cost/analytics; it blocks 9l5-class analytics but is not attached to the 9l5 epic and is only P2 while several dependents are also P2 — acceptable, but verify it is not scheduled after its consumers.
dep_priority.proposed: Keep P2; attach h6r + h10 under 9l5 (outcome-grounded analytics) so the calibration lane is scheduled as one epic slice.

reorg.rationale: P1 'Activation layer' is the adoption arm of the context/memory loop — it teaches agents to use exactly what 37t builds (preamble 37t.4, protocol 37t.2, judgment gate). Its own deps (pj8, d1y) are s7ae children, so s7ae is the alternative, but the bead's deliverable is standing CLAUDE.md context + adoption metrics for the memory loop = 37t. Highest-priority orphan; should not float.
Bug is 'full usage diagnostic D-stated on the live archive; 30s SQL equivalent existed' — a bug that only bites at real archive scale, which is 1xc's exact charter. Populates the empty P1 epic.
Archive schema hygiene for evidence-cockpit read paths (unsafe joins, JSON checks) is scale-read hardening; belongs with the scale-bug epic.
'Archive scaling doctrine — keep everything, optimize the ceilings' is the decision layer over 1xc's bug layer (both cite 16.5K/42GB growth). Co-locate the doctrine with the scale-hardening epic.
Self-tracing (daemon spans in its own archive) explicitly feeds 20d.14 latency projection and is daemon/perf observability; 20d ('Interactive performance') owns the latency/instrumentation program.

Perf-instrumentation doctrine (slow-query log, CLI phase timings, log discipline) is the continuous complement to 20d.7 EQP sweep and feeds bby.8/20d.14 acceptance; belongs to the interactive-performance epic.
'Walk the seven core agent flows end-to-end' is the empirical complement to the pj8 cookbook, and pj8 is an s7ae child; both are the coordination-substrate adoption layer.
'polylogue judge' is the human ergonomics of the judgment gate that is the heart of the 37t memory model; its own text says 37t.1 owns lifecycle semantics while this bead owns the surface.
Decision on user.db assertion vocabulary (epistemic vs curation vs workspace vs comms) is foundational to the claims->judgment->preamble loop; 37t is the epic that consumes the vocabulary.
Lesson-reinforcement scheduling (judged memory on a forgetting curve, SRS over injection) is a direct extension of the memory loop's injection stage.
'Blackboard as agent comms: cross-session messages that arrive' is multi-agent coordination messaging; deps include s7ae.3 and it is the delivery half of the coordination substrate.

'Express devloop state in Polylogue substrate' = write focus transitions/handoffs/proof-claims as work-event assertions, which is precisely rii ('Live substrate intake: agents write work-events; evidence materializes in-loop'). rii currently has only 2 children.
Prediction/calibration tracking (Brier, reliability buckets via 9l5.7) is outcome-grounded analytics; dep on 9l5.7 already makes it a 9l5 slice.

Experiment hosting (declared arms, preregistered metrics, paired analysis via the measure registry) depends on 9l5.7 and is analytics substrate; sits under the outcome-grounded analytics epic (relates-to 37t for context experiments).
Docs information architecture (tiered index, orphan/stale sweep) is core external legibility; 3tl.9 already owns docs coverage lint, this is its sibling.
Generated codebase atlas (self-grokking mechanical map) is a legibility deliverable feeding the README skim ladder (3tl.1).
Datasette lane ('your archive is just SQLite') targets exactly the validator/evangelist audience 3tl is about, and proves no-lock-in — external legibility.
Docs-site verification lane (pages cache + link integrity) is legibility infrastructure; pairs with 3tl.9 docs ownership.
'Docs/theming/release-proof/control-plane polish' is externally-inspectable polish = 3tl scope.

Codex deep integration (state_5.sqlite as authoritative source, mirror of fs1.1; app-server live lane) is the Codex analogue of the Hermes state.db bridge that fs1 owns. Alternatively anchors the proposed ecosystem-interop epic, but fs1's 'product-local state is authoritative; snapshots are fallback' pattern is an exact match.
'Harden blob hash validation + drop misleading symlink check' is blob-store integrity/hygiene = 83u ('Attachment & blob evidence integrity').
Consolidate the three session->HTML render paths = delete parallel dispatch, t46's charter.
Facade decomposition of the 5.2k-line 126-method God-object is the QA-middle-layer/parallel-dispatch removal t46 names.
'One status assembly' collapses three disagreeing status computations into one contract = t46 delete-parallel-dispatch.
Session changes view lands a web 'Changes' tab as evidence-cockpit content; bby ('from result list to evidence cockpit') is its home surface (also relates-to bby.11).
Browser-capture posting channel is web-capture/posting infrastructure the workbench (bby) surfaces; b1n (its dependent) is webui-driven posting.
WebUI-driven posting is UI plumbing over the ptx channel, delivered in the web workbench.
Cross-surface handoff ('polylogue open' + copy-as-command everywhere, one ref grammar) is exactly jnj ('Product surface algebra: one rule per concern across CLI/config/onboarding').
'Named bounded-dialogue layout' is a reusable read-view/render profile wired to DSL terminal stages (fnm.6); the query-DSL epic owns projection/layout naming.

reorg.to: Configuration doctrine & DB-backed runtime preferences (proposed epic)
reorg.rationale: y4c has 4 dependents, all orphaned (3xx, y8w, 6kh, 1jc), plus y4c itself is the doctrine spine. This is the single clearest missing-epic signal: a doctrine bead + a bundle of dependent implementation bundles with no umbrella. Adopt y4c (spine), 3xx (verb/ops prefs), y8w (reading prefs), 6kh (query-scope prefs), 1jc (learned defaults). Promote y4c to epic or create a new epic over the five.

reorg.to: Operational resilience: recoverable, forgettable-on-purpose, observable in prod (proposed epic)
reorg.rationale: A coherent ops/daemon-trust cluster with no epic: 4be (restore drill — untested backups), 27m (excision + secret hygiene), peo (daemon death forensics + heartbeat), s8q (deployed-state trust). Adjacent observability beads opc/oxz are alternatively here (I routed them to 20d as perf). All concern 'the archive stays trustworthy/recoverable/observable' which no current epic covers.

reorg.to: Ecosystem interop & origin breadth: two-way citizen (ingest + export lanes) (proposed epic)
reorg.rationale: Large unparented cluster whose beads literally describe being a 'two-way citizen': ingest breadth (t0p, uiw, 611, 2qx, 0cg, 7xv, ale, 7fj) and export/interop lanes (wmj, 7k7, r47, 4g5). ox0 (Codex state-db) belongs to the ingest side but I routed it to fs1. No epic currently owns origin/format breadth; the detector-registry work makes each mechanical, so an umbrella is warranted.

reorg.to: Substrate consolidation: kill the twins, God-modules, and duplicated primitives (proposed epic)
reorg.rationale: Internal-debt refactor cluster distinct from t46's surface-dispatch focus: 0aj (declared write-effects chain), yp0 (daemon internal event bus), 48h (10 copies of _table_exists), pf1 (sync/async twin divergence), 1ty (fts_freshness declared twice), 1a9 (dead stubs), dab (stop materializing run-projection cache), c9y (28-package boundary doctrine). Several are mutual siblings (0aj/yp0 both feed the daemon nervous system; pf1/48h/hiu are the storage-twin trap).

yeq (advanced verification lanes: metamorphic/chaos/contract-walks) and th0 (interactive-surface test harness: pty/completions/fzf) form a 2-bead 'verification depth' mini-cluster with no epic. Too small to justify a new epic; keep standalone but note they could seed a testing epic if more test-infra beads appear. Other clearly-standalone orphans: tsk, 30h, utf, 5dx, dx1, 0dz, lu1, gqx, c36, 2jj, 5en, 7fj-adjacent 611.
